extracted_codes.display: Renal biopsy; percutaneous, by trocar or needle
extracted_codes.context: Remove 50200 (Renal biopsy; percutaneous, by trocar or needle) from dialysis procedure CPT4 codes.

clinical_criteria: eGFR >= 90 (for CKD G1-Control and CKD G1A1-Control)
eGFR >= 90 AND (A2 or A3 or known kidney disease) for CKD Stage 1
eGFR 60 to less than 90 for CKD Stage 2
eGFR 45 to less than 60 for CKD Stage 3a
eGFR 30 to less than 45 for CKD Stage 3b
eGFR 15 to less than 30 for CKD Stage 4
eGFR < 15 for CKD Stage 5
UACR < 30 mg/g for A1 staging (Normal)
UACR 30-300 mg/g for A2 staging (moderately increased albuminuria)
UACR > 300 mg/g for A3 staging (severely increased albuminuria)
UPCR <= 0.19 g/g for A1 staging
UPCR > 0.19 & <= 0.85 g/g for A2 staging
UPCR > 0.85 g/g for A3 staging
UA protein Negative, Trace, or 10 for A1 staging
UA protein 30 for A2 staging
UA protein 100 or more for A3 staging
Daily urine albumin excretion > 30 mg/24 hours for albuminuria definition
Spot urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) > 30 mg/g for albuminuria definition
Co-occurrence defined as acute condition happening before or after 1 month (31 days) of eGFR
Urine protein test search window: 24 months (730 days) before most recent eGFR until present
CKD Stage 1 requires abnormalities present on most recent urine test and previous test obtained more than 3 months prior
algorithm_summary: The CKD phenotype algorithm follows NKF KDOQI and KDIGO guidelines to identify and stage chronic kidney disease using EHR data. It uses two main measures: estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) calculated with CKD-EPI equation for G-staging (Stages 1-5), and albuminuria for A-staging (A1-A3). The algorithm defines multiple case types: ESRD with transplant, ESRD on dialysis, and NKF CKD Stages 1-5, plus two control types (CKD G1-Control and CKD G1A1-Control). It excludes patients with acute conditions (AKI, sepsis, volume depletion, shock) occurring within 31 days of eGFR measurements and uses machine learning to predict A-staging from various urine tests. The algorithm addresses challenges with incomplete race/height data and longitudinal aspects by using structured rules for co-occurrence of acute conditions and specific time windows for urine test evaluation.
